page: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The processor is an Intel Xeon 6975P-C running at 3.9 GHz, equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache. It includes 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance has no local storage and contains zero GPUs. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 22 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the c8i.12xlarge demonstrates strong performance across multiple workloads. It achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing tests. Multi-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth tests show strong read-only performance for both small and large blocks, though write performance for large blocks remains in the average tier. Cryptographic and database operations also rank in the top 25%.

This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration, leveraging its dedicated CPU allocation and high-speed DDR5 memory. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, text and image processing, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference, where its advanced vector and matrix extensions can be fully utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory, a 480 MB L3 cache, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance includes no local storage and zero GPUs, with a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and image processing, alongside strong multi-core capabilities in software compilation and database operations. Its hardware profile makes it highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads, including static web serving, software build pipelines, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for compilation, database, and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring forty-eight vCPUs, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced vector extensions for intensive processing workloads.

96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with a 480 MB L3 cache
Top-tier single-threaded compression and image processing benchmark performance
No local storage and zero GPUs, relying on network-attached storage

Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
